ud3.10:1.2:
on one occasion, the Blessed One was staying at Uruvelā on the
bank of the Nerañjarā River at the root of the Bodhi tree–
the tree of awakening–newly awakened.
ud3.10:1.3:
And on that occasion he sat at the root of the Bodhi tree for seven days in one session,
sensitive to the bliss of release.
ud3.10:2.1:
Then, with the passing of seven days, after emerging from that concentration,
he surveyed the world with the eye of an Awakened One.
ud3.10:2.2: As he did so, he saw living beings burning with the many fevers and aflame with the many fires born of passion,
ud3.10:3.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, he on that occasion exclaimed:
ud3.10:4.2: Afflicted by contact, it calls disease a ‘self.’
ud3.10:5.1: Becoming otherwise, the world is attached to becoming,
ud3.10:5.2: afflicted by becoming, and yet delights in that very becoming.
ud3.10:5.3: Where there’s delight, there is fear.
ud3.10:6.1:
Whatever contemplatives or brahmans say that liberation from becoming is by means of becoming,
all of them are not released from becoming, I say.
ud3.10:6.2:
And whatever contemplatives or brahmans say that escape from becoming is by means of non-becoming,
all of them have not escaped from becoming, I say.
ud3.10:7.1:
For this stress comes into play in dependence on every acquisition.
With the ending of every clinging/sustenance, there’s no stress coming into play.
ud3.10:7.3: Beings, afflicted with thick ignorance, are unreleased from passion for what has come to be.
ud3.10:7.4: All levels of becoming, anywhere, in any way, are inconstant, stressful, subject to change.
ud3.10:8.1: Seeing this–as it’s come to be–
ud3.10:8.5: From the total ending of craving
ud3.10:8.6:
comes fading & cessation without remainder:
unbinding.
ud3.10:9.2: through lack of clinging/sustenance, there’s no further becoming.
ud3.10:9.3: He has conquered Māra, won the battle,
